predictions.description: Positional XPath /catalog/book[2]/title selects the SECOND book's title (1-based XPath indexing).
get-node works on the attribute axis (@id) — the string() wrap returns the attribute value.
get-node accepts XPath functions: count(/catalog/book) stringifies to '2'.
xml-get-children returns child ELEMENT tag names in document order (text nodes excluded).
xml-get-child-values returns child elements' text contents in document order — including '9.99' as TEXT (no numeric coercion).
xml-get-attribute reads @id from the element the XPath selects. The follow-up read of the MISSING @lang on book[2] fails loudly (buried) and must NOT overwrite this value.

xml-to-json (fast-xml-parser, defaults): elements become nested keys, @_-prefixed attributes kept, parseAttributeValue leaves 'b1' a string but element text '9.99' becomes JSON number 9.99, single child stays an object (no forced array).
xml-stringify (pretty=false) reproduces the parsed source byte-for-byte — no added declaration, attribute order and '5.50' text preserved.
